about.full:
15+ years of academic experience in Computer Science, Geo-AI, and XR technologies

Contributed as a senior researcher to large-scale national projects totaling over $9.3M in funding, collaborating under Sejong University principal investigators.

Published 200+ peer-reviewed papers in top-tier journals and int'l confs

Secured 51+ patents (US and Korean) in spatial analysis and XR technologies. 

Exploring culturally-grounded Physical AI: currently developing frameworks and prototypes for intelligent robots and XR agents capable of using geospatial awareness and affective signals to interpret and convey cultural context.

Initiating cross-cultural Geo-AI collaborations: leveraging multicultural experience to design future AI/VR research that bridges Korean and Middle Eastern cultural contexts through spatial and immersive intelligence.

Curriculum Innovation (in progress): proposing and designing specialized future courses on AI for Art & Culture, Embodied Cultural Intelligence, and GeoAI for Heritage — integrating technical AI skills with artistic, cultural, and cross-regional applications. 

Contributed in launching multiple research centers, including the XR Metaverse Research Center

Supervised 40+ Master's and 6+ Ph.D. students

Established international collaborations with institutions worldwide

Pioneered integration of AI with GIS, XR, and Physical/Embodied systems.

Received multiple prestigious awards including Australian Endeavour Fellowship

Demonstrated success in industry-academia collaboration

Overview

During the faculty position, he has been teaching various classes related to Computer Science and Engineering specially Virtual Reality, Mixed Reality, Geo-AI, Geo-informatics. He supervised more than 40 Master and six PhD students directly and indirectly during my 15 years research in various universities. Long time experiences in performing research and teaching in academic areas, makes me capable to perform a considerable contribution to postgraduate research student supervision. 

Planning and designing new graduate-level courses such as AI for Art and Culture, Embodied Cultural Intelligence, Physical GeoAI, and Affective XR for Cultural Experiences, aligning with the emerging focus on AI-driven creative technologies.

Academic Teaching Positions

Associate Professor | Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Sejong University, South Korea (March 2017 - Present)

Invited Lecturer | Department of GIS Engineering, Geomatics Engineering Faculty, KNTU (Summer Semester)

Teaching Areas

Delivered hands-on workshops and courses on Python and R programming for GIS, machine learning, and big data technologies.

Integrated tools like TensorFlow, Orange Data Mining, and Apache Spark into classroom learning.

Introduced hands-on sessions using LangChain for integrating NLP and LLM into real-world applications.

Expert in geospatial Python libraries for GIS applications and advanced geospatial computing.

Machine Learning & Deep Learning

Proficient in scikit-learn, TensorFlow, and Keras for supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning.

Experienced in developing neural networks for spatial and temporal data analysis.

Natural Language Processing (NLP) & Large Language Models (LLM)

Expertise in tools such as Hugging Face Transformers, LangChain, and Replicate for multimodal model integration.

Developed and deployed NLP applications for text generation, summarization, and semantic search.

Big Data Programming

Proficient in Apache Hadoop, MapReduce, Apache Spark, PySpark, SparkML, Spark SQL, and Spark MLlib.

Hands-on experience with NoSQL DBMS and ETL workflows for real-time analysis.

Data Analysis & Visualization

Advanced proficiency in Python libraries (Pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib, Seaborn) and R for statistical analysis.

Experienced in designing dashboards and visual systems for AI-driven cultural data exploration.

Web Development

Full-stack development experience using Django (backend) and HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript (frontend).

Extended Reality (XR) and Metaverse

Hands Hands-on experience in VR, AR, and MR application development, focusing on multimodal and affective interaction.

Applied XR systems for spatial storytelling and immersive cultural experiences.

Bioinformatics & Sensors

Integrated bio-sensing technologies (GSR, ECG, EEG, EMG) for affective computing and emotional state recognition.

Developed algorithms for physiological signal processing and emotion-aware user interfaces.

Culture Technology (CT)

Developed ontology-based and machine learning systems for cultural heritage analysis and semantic retrieval using AR/VR/GIS tools.

Contributed to AI-driven creative technology frameworks linking art, culture, and intelligent systems.

IoT & Ubiquitous Computing

Designed IoT-enabled ubiquitous systems integrating sensor networks and data fusion for real-time monitoring and smart cultural environments.

Human-Computer Interaction (HCI)

Created adaptive interfaces using gesture recognition, eye-tracking, and usability testing (SUS) to enhance user experience and interaction efficiency.

Research & Academic Skills

Research Leadership & Team Management

Academic Publishing & Scientific Writing

Grant Writing & Project Management

International Research Collaboration

Graduate Student Supervision

Global recognition as a Top 2% Scientist, reflecting extensive expertise impact in Geo-AI & XR tech

Curriculum Design and Innovation in AI for Art & Culture

Cross-cultural Academic Networking (Korea–Middle East–Global)

Domain Knowledge

Established Expertise

Multimodal AI (Vision, Audio, Language) for Art & Heritage

Affective Computing & Spatial Interaction Design

Cross-cultural GeoAI & XR-based Cultural Analytics

GeoAI-driven Decision-Making Systems

Human–Computer Interaction (HCI) & Spatial Media Systems

Smart City Technologies & Location-based Services

Deep expertise in geospatial analytics supported by active membership in the American Association of Geographers (AAG).

Emerging Research Focus

Physical AI & Embodied Cultural Intelligence

Integrating geospatial awareness, robotics, and affective computing for embodied cultural interpretation.

AI-driven Cultural Data Visualization (developing through ongoing projects and proposed interdisciplinary courses)

Creating interpretable AI tools to visualize cross-cultural and artistic datasets.

Cultural Robotics & Intelligent Agents for Art Performance (future interdisciplinary direction under Culture Technology framework)

Designing autonomous XR and robotic systems capable of performing and interpreting cultural narratives.
